input.title: Smart Home IoT Platform
input.description: Smart Home IoT Platform is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Node.js, MongoDB, MQTT, InfluxDB for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Device Registration and Pairing
requirements: Users must be able to complete device registration and pairing through the approved entry points provided by the platform.
The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing device registration and pairing to continue.
Device Registration and Pairing must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
The user interface for device registration and pairing should provide clear success, failure, and recovery messages for common scenarios.

FR-02.title: Room and Zone Management
requirements: The system must provide end-to-end room and zone management capabilities for creating, updating, viewing, and retiring relevant business records.
Room and Zone Management should support role-based permissions, validation rules, and duplicate-prevention checks where appropriate.
Users must be able to search, filter, and review historical activity related to room and zone management without leaving the module context.
The platform should maintain auditability for important actions performed within room and zone management and retain operational history for reporting.

FR-03.title: Device Control Dashboard
requirements: The system must provide device control dashboard with role-appropriate filters, date ranges, and drill-down views.
Device Control Dashboard should calculate metrics from current transactional data and keep summaries aligned with source records.
Authorized users must be able to export or share device control dashboard outputs in formats used by business teams.
The platform should retain query history, report configuration, or dashboard preferences where that improves repeated analysis.

FR-04.title: Light Control with Dimming
requirements: The system must support light control with dimming as a complete business workflow within the application.
Light Control with Dimming should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with light control with dimming from the relevant module.
The platform should log significant actions, failures, and user-visible events related to light control with dimming for operational support.

FR-01.title: Frontend Implementation of Device Registration and Pairing
FR-01.description: Implement the frontend behavior for device registration and pairing. Build the required user interactions, validation states, loading states, and error handling so the interface supports these behaviors: Users must be able to complete device registration and pairing through the approved entry points provided by the platform; The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing device registration and pairing to continue; Device Registration and Pairing must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.

FR-01.description: Implement the backend logic for device registration and pairing. Build the required APIs, business rules, validation, permissions, persistence, and processing needed to support these behaviors: Users must be able to complete device registration and pairing through the approved entry points provided by the platform; The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing device registration and pairing to continue; Device Registration and Pairing must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
